fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Oregon.

steps.title: Submit an Application
steps.description: Complete the Pharmacy Technician Registration application through the Oregon Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Pass a Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Complete a criminal background check as part of the application process.

steps.title: Complete Continuing Education
steps.description: Complete required continuing education hours prior to each registration renewal.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the PTCB. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Oregon pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: Oregon’s pharmacy technician application totals $115: a $70 application fee plus a $45 national fingerprint background check fee. The board’s renewal FAQ lists a $104 renewal total before late fees.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Oregon have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Oregon. To work in Oregon, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Oregon?
faqs.answer: The Oregon Board of Pharmacy says pharmacy technician applications usually take about 4 to 6 weeks to process once all materials are in.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Oregon while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: The Oregon Board of Pharmacy says pharmacy technician applications usually take about 4 to 6 weeks to process once all materials are in.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.
